rootPrinciple.canonicalPhraseNo: Alle mennesker er like mye verdt
rootPrinciple.interpretation: HAVEN development choices should preserve equal human worth, agency, dignity, privacy, and the ability to participate without hidden scoring or coercive profiling.

compatibility.changePolicy: This knowledge base keeps the current purpose://root ref. New branches marked candidate do not change runtime taxonomy until reviewed and ported.

statusMeanings.active: Already represented by the current CellScaffold purpose taxonomy or directly derived from it.
statusMeanings.draft: Documented as a durable HAVEN purpose, but not yet wired as canonical runtime taxonomy.
statusMeanings.candidate: Potential branch for review before runtime adoption.

nodeDefaults.goalRequirement: Every purpose node needs an achievable Goal with observable success signals and a verifier.
nodeDefaults.personalDataRule: Personal facts and preferences must be explicit, owner-scoped, inspectable, and deletable.

nodes.title: Alle mennesker er like mye verdt
nodes.summary: Top-level normative anchor for HAVEN purposes.

goal.outcome: HAVEN choices preserve equal worth, human agency, and explicit accountable purpose.
successSignals: New purposes can be traced to a child of purpose://root.
No accepted purpose requires hidden profiling, irreversible user lock-in, or unexplained ranking of people.
User-facing capabilities expose clear purpose, owner control, and reviewable consequences.
goal.verifier: PurposeKnowledgeBase review plus contract/security review for new canonical branches.

nodes.title: Human agency and understanding
nodes.summary: Help people understand, choose, delegate, review, and reverse digital actions.

goal.outcome: A person can understand what HAVEN is doing for them and can approve, pause, revoke, or revise meaningful actions.
successSignals: Sensitive actions have explicit confirmation or a documented grant.
User-facing summaries expose what will happen, what data is used, and what can be undone.
Human review queues aggregate similar decisions instead of forcing repetitive approvals.
goal.verifier: Action-plan risk review, consent checkpoints, and review-queue audit.

nodes.title: Contact and communication
nodes.summary: Help people initiate, continue, or refuse contact with explicit purpose and privacy boundaries.

goal.outcome: A person can communicate or be introduced without exposing private contact data outside consent and grants.
successSignals: Contact action is represented as draft, request, follow-up, or denied private data.
Private fields such as phone and email are not revealed without consent.
Recipient intent and allowed purpose are visible before sending.
goal.verifier: ContactEndpointCell tests, conference prompt fixtures, and privacy-boundary checks.

nodes.title: Contact introduction
nodes.summary: Introduce or connect people through a purpose-bound contact request.

goal.outcome: A contact introduction is prepared or delivered without leaking private contact information.
successSignals: Introduction has requester, intended recipient, purpose, and message draft.
Send/deliver requires explicit user action or grant.
Private contact data remains hidden unless explicitly consented.
goal.verifier: ContactEndpointCell and meeting-request prompt tests.

nodes.title: Contact follow-up
nodes.summary: Continue an existing contact or meeting thread with explicit recipient and message context.

goal.outcome: A follow-up message can be drafted for the correct recipient without auto-sending.
successSignals: Recipient is resolved or clarification is requested.
Message stays draft until explicit send action.
Previous context is cited when used.
goal.verifier: Contact follow-up and pronoun-resolution prompt fixtures.

nodes.title: Event participation
nodes.summary: Help a person participate in an event by understanding agenda, people, privacy, and next useful actions.

goal.outcome: A participant can decide what to attend, who to meet, and what to share without hidden pressure or data leakage.
successSignals: Agenda recommendations cite event context and time.
Meeting actions remain drafts until confirmed.
Privacy-sensitive requests deny private data or require consent.
goal.verifier: Conference prompt fixtures and event-surface canaries.

nodes.title: Event agenda recommendation
nodes.summary: Recommend or open an agenda item based on current time, interests, constraints, and available event context.

goal.outcome: The user gets a relevant agenda recommendation or a clear missing-context response.
successSignals: Recommendation includes session identity or time when known.
Missing room/person/context is acknowledged instead of invented.
Open-session action uses an explicit action keypath or remains read-only.
goal.verifier: Conference agenda prompt fixtures.

aliases: hva bør jeg få med meg

nodes.title: Event meeting draft request
nodes.summary: Draft an event meeting request or introduction while requiring explicit user action before sending.

goal.outcome: A relevant meeting or coffee request is drafted for the right person and never silently sent.
successSignals: Person and meeting intent are extracted or clarification is requested.
Draft state is visible before send.
Private contact fields are not exposed.
goal.verifier: Conference meeting-request and contact-introduction fixtures.

nodes.title: Event schedule change request
nodes.summary: Propose a schedule change for an event item while requiring role checks and confirmation.

goal.outcome: A schedule change is proposed or queued for approval without silently changing the event program.
successSignals: Target session or agenda item is identified or clarification is requested.
Role and confirmation requirements are visible.
No schedule mutation is committed without approval.
goal.verifier: Conference schedule-change fixtures and schedule mutation confirmation tests.

nodes.title: Privacy-preserving event networking
nodes.summary: Help people meet others at an event while limiting profile visibility and private data exposure.

goal.outcome: The user can network with consent controls and without exposing more profile data than intended.
successSignals: Privacy preference is explained or set as a draft action.
Aggregate-only or consented-only reports are distinguished from private data requests.
Private contact requests are denied or routed to consent workflow.
goal.verifier: Conference privacy, sponsor, and networking prompt fixtures.

nodes.title: Digital work
nodes.summary: Purposes that help people or scaffolds coordinate, collect, share, inspect, and improve digital work.

goal.outcome: Digital work surfaces make useful work easier without hiding state, purpose, or access boundaries.
successSignals: A user can identify the current task or artifact state.
Actions map to real cell keypaths or documented missing capabilities.
Work outputs can be inspected, shared, or tested according to their purpose.
goal.verifier: Cell contract checks, action-plan coverage, and GUI smoke tests.

nodes.title: Coordinate work
nodes.summary: Plan, coordinate, share, and track work across people, agents, or scaffolds.

goal.outcome: People and scaffolds can see who is doing what, what is blocked, and what needs a decision.
successSignals: Project/task state exposes open, blocked, done, and awaiting-review counts.
Sharing paths preserve recipient access boundaries.
Outstanding decisions can be queued for a human or delegated agent.
goal.verifier: ProjectPortfolioCell, WorkItemCell, share-flow, and review-queue tests.

nodes.title: Project work overview and sharing
nodes.summary: Manage projects, tasks, sharing, and current outstanding work.

goal.outcome: A user can share desired project intent and see current status plus outstanding work.
successSignals: ProjectPortfolio.state can expose status, health, priority, milestones, and counts.
WorkItem.state can expose open, blocked, in-progress, done, comments, subtasks, and relations.
IdeaTaskWorkspace/IdeaTaskShare can prepare, deliver, and open a selected snapshot for a recipient.
goal.verifier: ExecutablePurposeGroundingCellTests, ProjectPortfolioCellTests, WorkItemCellTests, and share-flow contract tests.

nodes.title: Project portfolio
nodes.summary: Track project status, health, priorities, milestones, releases, and capacity.

goal.outcome: Project status, health, priority, and milestone/release state can be read and counted.
successSignals: ProjectPortfolio.state exposes openCount, activeCount, blockedCount, health counts, roadmapTable, and milestoneTable.
goal.verifier: ProjectPortfolioCellTests and ProjectPortfolio.state assertions.

nodes.title: Work items
nodes.summary: Track tasks, bugs, decisions, comments, subtasks, relations, and blockers.

goal.outcome: Open, blocked, in-progress, and done work can be tracked with relations and supporting evidence.
successSignals: WorkItem.state exposes total/open/blocked counts, board lanes, dependency risk, and selected item detail.
goal.verifier: WorkItemCellTests and WorkItem.state assertions.

nodes.title: Share selected project intent
nodes.summary: Prepare, deliver, and open a selected project/work snapshot for a recipient.

goal.outcome: A selected idea/work snapshot can be prepared, delivered, and opened by an admitted recipient.
successSignals: IdeaTaskWorkspace.prepareShareInvite returns a preview.
IdeaTaskWorkspace.deliverShareInvite records delivery state.
IdeaTaskShare.acceptInvitationAccess opens accepted shared state for the recipient.
goal.verifier: IdeaTaskWorkspace and IdeaTaskShare share-flow tests.

nodes.title: Current status and outstanding work
nodes.summary: Show where a project stands now and what remains outstanding.

goal.outcome: The user can see current project position and outstanding work in one overview.
successSignals: ProjectPortfolio.state and WorkItem.state expose matching open/blocked/risk/upcoming counts and query rows.
The overview identifies waiting-on-human and waiting-on-cell states separately.
goal.verifier: Combined ProjectPortfolio/WorkItem state assertions and GUI smoke.

nodes.title: Collect structured input
nodes.summary: Collect structured answers or evidence from people or systems.

goal.outcome: Structured input can be authored, delivered, collected, inspected, and exported with consent and evidence boundaries.
successSignals: Input schema is visible.
Submission state is explicit.
Responses can be read or exported according to grants.
goal.verifier: QuestionnaireCampaignCell tests and response/export assertions.

nodes.title: Review content before publish
nodes.summary: Prepare publication of notes, summaries, or public content with review, consent, and explicit confirmation.

goal.outcome: Content can be reviewed and approved before any publish action happens.
successSignals: Target content and publication destination are identified or clarification is requested.
Privacy-sensitive or personal content is reviewed before publication.
No content is marked published without explicit confirmation.
goal.verifier: Published-content review fixtures and publish-action confirmation tests.

nodes.title: Complete questionnaire campaign
nodes.summary: Create, deliver, answer, inspect, and analyze a questionnaire campaign.

goal.outcome: A user can create, deliver, answer, inspect, and analyze a consent-gated questionnaire.
successSignals: Respondent form opens, saves draft, submits, and records consent state.
Admin authoring can configure questions, consent, pages, and lifecycle.
Responses can be collected, read, exported, and analyzed without leaking invitation payloads.
goal.verifier: QuestionnaireCampaignCell tests, PromptPurposeLab fixtures, and GUI smoke.

nodes.title: Respondent questionnaire form
nodes.summary: Expose consent, questions, draft save, submit, and withdrawal paths for a respondent.

goal.outcome: Respondent can answer and submit only after consent.
successSignals: Respondent open link returns the expected campaign context.
Draft save persists answers without marking final submission.
Submit records submitted state, consent state, and receipt ID.
goal.verifier: QuestionnaireCampaign respondent flow tests.

nodes.title: Questionnaire admin authoring
nodes.summary: Configure title, context, consent, questions, pages, and lifecycle.

goal.outcome: Admin can configure a campaign without editing code.
successSignals: Campaign settings setters update state.
Questions can be added, edited, ordered, and validated.
Admin can preview lifecycle and response policy before launch.
goal.verifier: QuestionnaireCampaign admin setter and authoring tests.

nodes.title: Questionnaire delivery and consent
nodes.summary: Select recipients, send access-proof links, and preserve consent boundaries.

goal.outcome: Only selected reachable recipients receive access-proof invitations.
successSignals: Delivery preview reports ready and blocked recipients.
Invitation payload avoids leaking questionnaire content.
Consent text and withdrawal boundary are visible before submission.
goal.verifier: Questionnaire delivery tests and transport payload policy.

nodes.title: Collect questionnaire responses
nodes.summary: Store draft and submitted answers with consent state and receipt IDs.

goal.outcome: Responses are persisted separately from invitation transport.
successSignals: Submitted answers appear only after respondent submit.
Draft answers remain distinguishable from submitted answers.
Consent and receipt evidence are queryable according to grants.
goal.verifier: QuestionnaireResponse and QuestionnaireCampaign tests.

nodes.title: Analyze questionnaire responses
nodes.summary: Inspect counts and export response data transparently.

goal.outcome: Admin can read, export, and analyze response data according to consent and grants.
successSignals: Response counts are visible.
Export JSON/CSV includes only permitted fields.
Analysis summaries cite the response set and filters used.
goal.verifier: Questionnaire response export and analysis tests.

nodes.title: Functional accessible GUI
nodes.summary: Ensure controls are labeled, focusable, visually stable, aesthetic, and wired to real actions.

goal.outcome: All visible controls work, text and fields do not overlap, and keyboard/focus behavior is usable.
successSignals: Buttons trigger real keypaths or explicit disabled states.
Text fields can receive input and persist or validate it.
Browser/Binding smoke passes without overlap in required viewports.
goal.verifier: Skeleton validation, GUI smoke, keyboard/focus checks, and screenshot inspection.

nodes.title: Owner entity access affordance
nodes.summary: Ensure every production skeleton leaves the owner a visible route back to their own entity, Co-Pilot, or equivalent recovery interface.

goal.outcome: A generated or user-authored skeleton cannot lock the owner out of their entity context.
successSignals: The production skeleton contains a visible Button, Reference, chat/composer, or equivalent interface affordance for owner entity / Co-Pilot access, or the host shell documents that it supplies one outside the portable skeleton.
The affordance binds to a real owner-scoped Cell/keypath or shell action and is not only decorative text.
Validation fails or warns before publication when the affordance is missing.
goal.verifier: skeleton_explore_validator.py with --require-owner-access, Binding CellConfigurationValidationService diagnostics, and renderer smoke that confirms the affordance is reachable.

aliases: brukeren må alltid nå sin entitet

nodes.title: Quality
nodes.summary: Cross-cutting quality purposes that support user-facing and scaffold-facing work.

goal.outcome: Capabilities are reliable, understandable, useful, and maintainable.
successSignals: Contracts exist for key behavior.
Failures are observable and recoverable.
Quality gates are tied to the purpose they protect.
goal.verifier: Contract tests, runtime probes, and documented acceptance criteria.

nodes.title: Governance
nodes.summary: Cross-cutting privacy, access, audit, preference, and owner-control purposes.

goal.outcome: Access and state changes are explicit, bounded, auditable, and owner-respecting.
successSignals: Sensitive reads and writes require visible grants or confirmations.
Preference and entity facts are owner-scoped and deletable.
Audit trails explain what changed and why.
goal.verifier: Resolver grant tests, audit-feed checks, and privacy review.

nodes.title: Access, audit and privacy
nodes.summary: Keep visibility grant-bound and mutations auditable.

goal.outcome: Reads, writes, shares, and exports follow explicit grants and leave reviewable evidence.
successSignals: Resolver denies missing grants.
Sensitive action plans identify risk level and confirmation needs.
Audit/feed entries explain successful mutations.
goal.verifier: Resolver policy tests and flow/audit assertions.

nodes.title: Questionnaire access audit
nodes.summary: Keep questionnaire responses consent-gated and transport payloads minimal.

goal.outcome: Admin visibility follows consent and invitation transport does not leak questionnaire content.
successSignals: Privacy policy state is visible.
Access decisions are represented in audit/feed events.
Invitation payloads contain access proof rather than response content.
goal.verifier: Privacy gate and invitation content tests.

nodes.title: Owner-controlled preferences
nodes.summary: Keep reusable preference facts explicit, scoped, inspectable, and deletable.

goal.outcome: HAVEN can reuse owner-approved preference evidence without hidden profiling.
successSignals: Reusable preferences are stored as explicit evidence records.
Perspective may weight active context but does not silently mutate owner facts.
The owner can delete or revise stored preference records.
goal.verifier: Entity/Perspective mutation tests and owner preference review.

nodes.title: Validation
nodes.summary: Purposes that prove behavior through acceptance, contract, replay, GUI, and regression checks.

goal.outcome: HAVEN can tell whether a purpose has been achieved or is blocked.
successSignals: Each canonical purpose has at least one measurable Goal.
Goal evaluations return achieved, at_risk, blocked, missed, or unknown with evidence.
Regression tests protect deterministic purpose decomposition.
goal.verifier: GoalEvaluationCell tests, contract probes, and prompt fixtures.

nodes.title: Acceptance tests
nodes.summary: Prove that a purpose can be achieved and stays stable.

goal.outcome: A purpose has executable checks that demonstrate the user-facing or scaffold-facing outcome.
successSignals: Relevant unit/contract tests pass.
Fixtures cover positive, negative, and ambiguous prompts where applicable.
GUI smoke exists for user-facing surfaces.
goal.verifier: Swift test filters, npm prompt-purpose audits, and browser canaries where relevant.

nodes.title: Purpose decomposition acceptance
nodes.summary: Protect deterministic prompt decomposition and model-proposal boundaries.

goal.outcome: Equivalent prompts produce stable purpose coverage, and model proposals cannot override HAVEN validation.
successSignals: Known fixtures pass deterministic resolver checks.
Ambiguous prompts ask for clarification or candidate review.
Hallucinated capabilities are rejected or moved to missing.
goal.verifier: ExecutablePurposeGroundingCellTests, PurposeKnowledge evaluator, and PromptPurposeLab audits.

nodes.title: Hallucination evaluation gate
nodes.summary: Periodically test that chat, purpose decomposition, model-assisted proposals, and source-backed answers do not invent facts, refs, capabilities, or unsupported certainty.

goal.outcome: HAVEN can periodically pass a hallucination evaluation suite and report regressions before hallucinated claims become accepted knowledge or user-facing behavior.
successSignals: Hallucinated purposeRefs, goalRefs, capability refs, keypaths, and source citations are rejected or reported as missing.
Unsupported factual claims are either grounded with checked sources, clearly marked as inference, or answered as unknown.
Known negative, adversarial, stale-source, and ambiguous fixtures do not become false canonical purposes.
When the target threshold has been met, later runs preserve the quality band instead of optimizing past it with regressions elsewhere.
goal.verifier: PurposeKnowledge evaluator, model-output scorer, citation/source audit, adversarial prompt fixtures, and sampled human review.

nodes.title: Questionnaire acceptance tests
nodes.summary: Protect questionnaire decomposition and campaign behavior.

goal.outcome: Questionnaire prompts produce stable purpose coverage and campaign contracts keep working.
successSignals: Questionnaire positive, negative, and ambiguous fixtures pass.
QuestionnaireCampaign admin/respondent/export tests pass.
GUI smoke covers admin and respondent surfaces.
goal.verifier: ExecutablePurposeGroundingCellTests, QuestionnaireCampaignCellTests, and GUI smoke.

nodes.title: Project work acceptance tests
nodes.summary: Protect project/work decomposition and cell behavior.

goal.outcome: Project/work prompts produce stable purpose coverage and existing cells keep their contracts.
successSignals: Project/work positive, negative, and ambiguous fixtures pass.
ProjectPortfolioCellTests and WorkItemCellTests pass.
Share-flow and overview tests cover the requested user workflow.
goal.verifier: ExecutablePurposeGroundingCellTests, ProjectPortfolioCellTests, WorkItemCellTests, and share-flow checks.

nodes.title: Knowledge and methodology
nodes.summary: Purposes that provide source evidence, freshness, methods, and reusable knowledge for decisions.

goal.outcome: HAVEN decisions can cite current internal state, stable local knowledge, and checked external sources when needed.
successSignals: Source records include path or URL, checked date, status, and applies-to fields.
Stale or unfamiliar external claims require a fresh source check.
Machine-readable knowledge is versioned and traceable to documents or code.
goal.verifier: Documentation/RAG audit and source freshness checks.

nodes.title: Current methodology sources
nodes.summary: Use source evidence with checked dates and stale-source policy.

goal.outcome: Claims about outside practice, law, standards, or model/runtime behavior are based on source records that can be rechecked.
successSignals: External source records have URL, domain, checkedAt, and status.
Unstable facts trigger browsing or official-source checks.
Source evidence is attached to the purpose node it supports.
goal.verifier: SourcePolicy checks and RAG/source audit.

nodes.title: Explain policy or rules
nodes.summary: Explain rules, policy, or consent boundaries in plain language without inventing authority.

goal.outcome: The user receives a clear explanation of the relevant rule and what actions are allowed, blocked, or require consent.
successSignals: Explanation names the relevant policy or missing source.
Plain-language mode is supported without condescension.
The explanation distinguishes aggregate, consented, and private data.
goal.verifier: Policy explanation fixtures and source-grounding checks.

nodes.title: Grounding
nodes.summary: Purposes that map prompts to executable, verified, side-effect-aware action plans.

goal.outcome: A prompt can be decomposed into purpose nodes, achievable Goals, required capabilities, missing pieces, and safe action plans.
successSignals: grounding.decompose returns stable purpose coverage for equivalent inputs.
Unknown capabilities are reported as missing instead of hallucinated.
Side effects require explicit action plans and grants.
goal.verifier: ExecutablePurposeGroundingCellTests and prompt-purpose audits.

nodes.title: Local model assisted decomposition
nodes.summary: Use a local model as proposal engine while HAVEN keeps deterministic authority.

goal.outcome: A local model may suggest templates and hints without mutating final purpose trees or hidden profile state.
successSignals: modelPolicy.mode=proposal falls back deterministically when unavailable.
Model output can only select templates, propose hints, or expose rejected claims.
Final PurposeNode output is built by HAVEN templates and validators.
goal.verifier: Stubbed LocalLLM tests, invalid JSON tests, and no Perspective/Entity mutation tests.

nodes.title: Capture and place new purposes
nodes.summary: Detect new purpose candidates, find their nearest ancestor, and queue review when automatic placement is uncertain or consent is required.

goal.outcome: New purpose candidates are captured, deduplicated, placed under the nearest known ancestor, and escalated only when needed.
successSignals: Candidate has a proposed parent, confidence, evidence, and missing capability list.
High-confidence safe placements are staged automatically without changing canonical taxonomy.
Consent-sensitive or low-confidence placements enter a human review accumulator.
goal.verifier: Purpose intake accumulator tests and taxonomy review queue audit.

nodes.title: Scaffold operations
nodes.summary: Purposes for scaffolds to keep themselves connected, observable, recoverable, and aligned with HAVEN.

goal.outcome: A scaffold can detect whether operational purposes are achieved, at risk, or blocked.
successSignals: Health checks emit events when network or peer contact changes.
PerspectiveCell can react to operational state changes as contextual signals.
Recovery helpers are selected by purpose and capability, not hidden scripts.
goal.verifier: Health/ping GoalEvaluation tests and scaffold event replay.

nodes.title: Personal semantic context state
nodes.summary: Represent owner-defined labels such as Home, Work, or lunch time without exposing raw location/time unless explicitly granted.

goal.outcome: Personal context labels become active, approaching, or ended based on owner-approved observations and tolerances.
successSignals: SemanticLocation label is evaluated from private location evidence with freshness and precision bounds.
SemanticTime label is evaluated in the owner's timezone with clear active windows.
Composite context Goals can combine labels without leaking raw observations.
goal.verifier: Semantic location/time cells, GoalEvaluationCell, and privacy-bound event tests.

aliases: Send event når jeg nærmer meg Hjemme

nodes.title: Value and commons
nodes.summary: Purposes for returning value, protecting commons, and avoiding extractive platform dynamics.

goal.outcome: Value flows are transparent, bounded by policy, and do not undermine equal human worth or owner agency.
successSignals: Credits, payouts, grants, or value-return mechanisms have explicit policy and regulatory review.
Contribution evidence is inspectable and contestable.
No user is reduced to an opaque monetization score.
goal.verifier: DiMy policy review, regulatory guardrail review, and value-flow audit.

nodes.title: Unknown prompt purpose
nodes.summary: Fallback when a prompt has no known canonical purpose yet.

goal.outcome: Unknown prompts are handled safely by reporting missing coverage, candidate purposes, and review needs.
successSignals: No false canonical purpose is asserted.
Candidate placement includes nearest known ancestor and confidence.
Human review is queued when needed.
goal.verifier: Negative and ambiguous prompt fixtures.

matchingIndexPlan.target: Faster candidate selection than full sparse cosine for common purpose routing.
normalization: Lowercase and ASCII-fold incoming text for index keys while retaining original text for explanation.
Map canonical purposeRefs and aliases through a hash map before lexical scoring.
Keep Norwegian and English aliases on the same node when HAVEN users commonly mix terms.

indexes.name: canonicalRefMap
indexes.use: O(1) canonical purposeRef and alias lookup.

indexes.name: tokenInvertedIndex
indexes.use: Shortlist candidate nodes from prompt tokens, capability names, interests, and visible keypaths.

indexes.name: ancestorIntervalIndex
indexes.use: O(1) ancestor checks for single-parent tree nodes.

indexes.name: ancestorBitsetIndex
indexes.use: Fast lowest-common-ancestor and shared-heritage checks with bitwise AND and deepest-set-bit lookup for small/medium taxonomies.

indexes.name: facetIndex
indexes.use: Attach cross-cutting GUI, privacy, source, and validation purposes without distorting the main tree ancestor.

indexes.name: goalVerifierIndex
indexes.use: Route from a matched purpose to the evidence needed to test achievement.

rankingOrder: High-signal token matches from node aliases and goal success signals.
Ancestor depth preference for specific nodes.
Sparse cosine or embedding rerank only over the top-K shortlist.

lcaStrategy.smallTaxonomy: Use ancestor bitsets and precomputed depth arrays.
lcaStrategy.largeTaxonomy: Use Euler tour entry/exit for isAncestor plus binary lifting for LCA.
lcaStrategy.crossCuttingFacets: Exclude facets from primary LCA, then report all-purpose LCA separately.
